page_content:
Formerly: PUAF717.

PLCY720 International Security Policy (3 Credits)

Reviews the principal features of international security as currently practiced. Traces the evolution of contemporary policy beginning

with the initiation of nuclear weapons programs during World War II. Particular emphasis is given to experience of the United States and Russia, since the historical interaction between these two countries has disproportionately affected the international security conditions that all other countries now experience.

Restriction: Must be in a major in PLCY-School of Public Policy; or permission of PLCY-School of Public Policy.

Credit Only Granted for. PLCY720 or PUAF720.
